title: lithocarpus lampongus
intro: Lithocarpus lampongus, also known as the Sumatran tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the island of Sumatra in Indonesia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits of Lithocarpus lampongus include its ability to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Lithocarpus lampongus can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, typically made by steeping the bark or leaves in hot water, or in the form of a tincture, which is often used in traditional medicine.
